inputs.price_source: Yahoo Finance WY close 2026-07-10 (mechanical refresh will sync)
inputs.fcf_source: Normalized Adjusted FAD: FY2024 $567M ($0.78/sh on 728M shares) haircut ~17% for sustained Wood Products weakness (Adjusted EBITDA $250M vs $661M FY2024) and lower log prices; FY2025 spot Adjusted FAD $397M ($0.55/sh) is trough (10-K 2026-02-13 Adjusted FAD reconciliation)
inputs.book_per_share_caveat: Stockholders equity $9,426M / 723.2M shares; timberlands at depletion cost ($11,533M) not fair-value NAV
inputs.dividend_source: Base quarterly dividend $0.18/sh ($0.72 annual) plus variable supplemental under 75-80% Adjusted FAD framework (10-K cash return framework)
inputs.acres_source: 10-K Item 1: more than 10 million acres U.S.; long-term licenses covering more than 14 million acres Canada
inputs.normalization_note: Lawrence base uses normalized Adjusted FAD, not GAAP EPS ($0.45 FY2025) or lumpy timberland sale gains ($266M FY2025 per MD&A)

optionality_gate.floor_value_note: GAAP equity ~$13.04/sh at 2025-12-31; timberlands at depletion basis ~$1,153/acre on books vs transaction comps often higher
optionality_gate.overlay_nav_note: [Assumption] timberland fair value ~$1,800/acre on 10M acres less ~$7/sh net debt; not GAAP book
optionality_gate.notes: Price ~80% above GAAP book; stance gate uses normalized Adjusted FAD ten-year path, not book floor alone

gaap_vs_fair_value.policy_source: 10-K Note 1: timber and timberlands at cost, less depletion

not_in_model_requires_refresh: Per-acre fair marks on 10M-acre portfolio vs Virginia Feb 2026 sale ($1,778/acre on 108k acres)
Monticello TimberStrand EWP capacity uplift from 2027 operations
Mid-cycle normalized lumber pricing for Wood Products segment
Carbon solutions and renewable energy lease monetization beyond run-rate

segments_or_options.label: 10M+ acre U.S. timberlands (West, South, North)
segments_or_options.evidence: FY2025 Adjusted EBITDA $581M; 6.7M acres Southern U.S.

segments_or_options.label: Real Estate & ENR (HBU, energy, natural resources)
segments_or_options.evidence: FY2025 Adjusted EBITDA $411M; Virginia 108k-acre sale $192M Q1 2026

segments_or_options.label: Wood Products (lumber, OSB, EWP mills)
segments_or_options.evidence: FY2025 Adjusted EBITDA $250M vs $661M FY2024 at lumber trough; net sales $4.957B

segments_or_options.label: Monticello TimberStrand facility (~$500M, ops 2027)
segments_or_options.evidence: 10-K MD&A: $500M investment, +10M cubic feet EWP capacity, $109M capex in 2025 excluded from Adjusted FAD

segments_or_options.label: 14M+ acres Canadian long-term licenses
segments_or_options.nav_assumption: Licensed harvest rights not fully on U.S. timber book

segments.label: Real Estate & ENR
segments.evidence: Lumpy HBU and ENR monetization; Q1 2026 Virginia sale gain $58M

segments.label: Wood Products
segments.evidence: Cyclical mill segment at lumber trough; positive EBITDDA but down from $661M FY2024

options.label: Monticello engineered wood products capacity
options.base_terminal_rationale: P_base 0.6 x incremental EWP cash flow from 2027 startup

options.label: Higher-and-better-use land bank
options.base_terminal_rationale: P_base 0.45 x incremental HBU value above normalized timber run-rate
options.evidence: FY2025 timberland sale gains $266M; Strategic Land Solutions segment

segment_build.corporate_drag_note: Interest on $5.57B debt (5.11% weighted avg), unallocated items ($221M Adjusted EBITDA drag), corporate G&A

bear.notes: Prolonged lumber trough; Wood Products EBITDA stays depressed; leverage limits buybacks; spot FAD ~$0.55/sh persists

base.notes: Normalized Adjusted FAD $0.65/sh; lumber cycle normalizes from FY2025 trough; Monticello ops 2027

bull.notes: Lumber rebound + HBU sales; Adjusted FAD $0.80+ mid-cycle; multiple re-rate; $938M buyback authorization deployed

human_review.notes: First filing-grounded dive. Normalized Adjusted FAD excludes Monticello growth capex and one-time pension settlement ($145M). Leverage rose to $5.57B in 2025 (term loan issuances).

growth_explanation.base_mechanism: Sustainable harvest on 10M+ U.S. acres; integrated Wood Products internal fiber; periodic HBU/ENR land sales; lumber cycle normalization from 2025 trough; Monticello EWP from 2027
growth_explanation.bear_risk: Wood Products cyclicality ($250M vs $661M Adjusted EBITDA); $5.57B long-term debt; housing starts softness; log price pressure
growth_explanation.source: 10-K 2026-02-13 segment tables and Adjusted FAD reconciliation

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: Timber at depletion cost $11.5B (~$1,153/acre); Virginia sale implied ~$1,778/acre Feb 2026

option_scan.question: Undeveloped reserves / acreage?
option_scan.evidence: 10M+ U.S. acres plus 14M Canadian licenses; Strategic Land Solutions and HBU real estate not fully in run-rate FAD

option_scan.question: In-business loss segment?
option_scan.evidence: Wood Products still generated $250M Adjusted EBITDA FY2025 despite lumber trough

option_scan.question: Backlog / contracted revenue not in FCF path?
option_scan.evidence: Monticello EWP facility ops target 2027; ENR lease contracts

option_scan.question: Private stakes below fair value?
option_scan.evidence: No material private equity stakes disclosed

option_scan.question: Transitory distribution?
option_scan.evidence: Base dividend $0.72/sh; supplemental variable under 75-80% Adjusted FAD framework; FY2025 FAD $0.55/sh below dividend

option_scan.question: Embedded in revenue?
option_scan.evidence: Timber harvest and mill sales in operating segments ($6.9B net sales FY2025)

paths.label: Segment-derived growth (pre-falsifier)
paths.weight_why: Segment blend before falsifier pass; diagnostic only when it differs from falsifier-adjusted.
weight_falsifiers: Included at full weight alongside falsifier-adjusted without divergence note

paths.label: Bear scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Downside stress; low weight because bear is a sensitivity, not a competing explanation of base mechanics.
weight_falsifiers: Bear assumptions become base case in filings (e.g. sustained volume collapse)
paths.hard_to_vary: yes: asymmetric low weight is structural

paths.label: Bull scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Upside stress probe; higher than bear because optionality archetype has skew, but still below primary paths.
weight_falsifiers: Bull growth sustained in filings for 4+ quarters without falsifier trigger

paths.label: NAV overlay dated payoff (7yr to overlay base)
paths.weight_why: Answers a different question (NAV convergence) than 7yr cash-flow IRR; capped weight to avoid theory conflation.
weight_falsifiers: NAV overlay base not updated when segment build or filings change >10%
floor_pass false but weight above 15% without [HUMAN REVIEW]

flags: NAV overlay is a different theory (asset payoff vs cash-flow IRR); weight capped at 10% to limit theory conflation.

deutsch_checks.reach: Weights encode evidence tier (A primary → D external), not fitted to hit a target IRR

synthesis.notes: Capstone IRR combining filings, segments, overlays, third-party, qualitative

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

context_overlay.disclaimer: Context only. Tailwinds inform stance and overlay sizing; they do not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion to base case requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

insider_signal.disclaimer: Context only. Insider activity informs scenario confidence and stance discussion; it does not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

scenario_confidence.note: Scenario weights are qualitative confidence only; Lawrence base IRR unchanged.

lens_consensus.disclaimer: Advisory lens blend only. Does not replace Lawrence total-synthesis stance gate without [HUMAN REVIEW].
